items.headline: South Korea economy grows 1.7% in first quarter, strongest in nearly six years
items.body: South Korea's economy expanded 1.7% in the first quarter, driven by a 5.1% surge in exports of semiconductors for artificial intelligence. The growth rate was the strongest since 2020 and beat forecasts by 70%.

items.headline: US military intercepts Iranian oil tankers in Asian waters far from Persian Gulf
items.body: The US intercepted at least three Iranian tankers near India, Malaysia and Sri Lanka, seizing vessels in international waters thousands of miles from the Strait of Hormuz. The remote locations suggest the US is avoiding mine-deployment risks near Iranian coastlines.

items.headline: Satellite images show major oil spills in Persian Gulf after strikes on Iranian facilities
items.body: Oil slicks from strikes on Lavan Island and other facilities now threaten desalination plants serving nearly 100 million people. Spills have reached protected wildlife refuges and could contaminate filtering systems for regional drinking water.

items.headline: Federal grand jury indicts Southern Poverty Law Center on fraud and money laundering charges
items.body: The SPLC faces 11 counts alleging it funneled over $3 million in donations to individuals linked to the Ku Klux Klan and neo-Nazi groups between 2014 and 2023. The indictment marks a major legal challenge to one of America's most prominent civil rights monitoring organizations.

no.headline: Norway takes state control of Europe's largest rare earth deposit to accelerate development
no.body: Government assumed planning authority for the Fen deposit after resources nearly doubled to 15.9 million metric tons. The move bypasses local objections and targets production by 2031 to reduce European dependence on China.

uk.headline: Cabinet Office chief says sacked Foreign Office leader refused to hand over Mandelson vetting files
uk.body: Cabinet Office permanent secretary told Parliament she held multiple discussions with Olly Robbins over file release before taking the unusual step of requesting documents directly from security officials. The refusal deepens institutional fractures days before elections projected to devastate Labour in former strongholds.
